result:
1. Config: add per-route body limit. New file config/body_size_limits.json (or extend rate_limits.json) mapping route→bytes; default stays 2MB, POST /v1/media/upload-meta=524288. Keeps code generic, config hot-reloadable, no CODEOWNERS gate (body size guard not in the approval list per Section 6).
2. Code: body_size_guard middleware lookup. src/edge/pipeline.ts step 4 currently flat 2MB. Change guard to read per-route ceiling by method+route prefix, fallback to 2MB. Must match after route resolution — but dispatcher is step 6. Fix: pre-match route key inline, or move route match earlier (cheaper: inline prefix match in guard).
3. Code: 413 error body shape. Must stay RFC 7807 per Section 2 rule 6: type=/errors/payload-too-large, title, status=413, detail="Max body 512KB for this route", instance=request_id. No internal stacktrace/version.
4. Unit tests: body_size_guard.test.ts. Cases: 512000B allowed, 524289B rejected on upload-meta; 600KB still allowed on other POST routes; non-upload-meta media routes unaffected. Assert RFC 7807 shape + request_id present.
5. Integration test: tests/integration/ against real Redis+PG (Section 11 — no DB mocks). Send 600KB multipart to POST /v1/media/upload-meta, assert 413 + metric increment + audit log line. Send 500KB, assert 2xx reaches stub downstream.
6. Observability: new metric atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route,method}. Add to src/edge/metrics.ts next to atlas_edge_rate_limit_total. Backfill dashboard panel on main Atlas board. Alert threshold: rejection rate >5% of upload-meta traffic for 10m → #oncall-platform (SEV3, Slack-only per Section 7).
7. Observability: keep audit_log step 9 line for each 413 with truncated content-length, route, trustedClientIp, request_id. Needed for abuse forensics. PII redaction already covers bodies (not logged).
8. Rollout: config-only change deployable without image rebuild if hot-reload exists; otherwise follow Section 6 blue/green — us-east-1 canary 5% 30min → eu-west-1 → ap-southeast-1, 10-min soak. Watch atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route="/v1/media/upload-meta"} and 5xx rate during canary.
9. Rollback: revert the config entry (PR revert or config-repo revert), no binary rollback needed. If hot-reload: single config push, <1min. Auto-rollback trigger: Section 6 SEV1 any hour. Manual trigger: rejection-rate alert + legitimate user reports in #oncall-platform.
10. Comms — partners: notify legacy X-Api-Key integrators (Section 5B) 7 days pre-rollout; they are likeliest to exceed the new limit. Channel: existing partner migration thread + changelog entry.
11. Comms — internal: media-svc team (owners of upload-meta semantics), security (since DoS-driven, they approved mitigation scope), #oncall-platform (runbook update: add 413 uptick scenario under Section 8 "5xx spike"—though 413 is 4xx, add sibling stanza).
12. Docs: update PROJECT HANDBOOK Section 3 (step 4 now per-route) and Section 10 incident history stub once abuse case is closed. Add lint note if any lint rule currently assumes 2MB constant.
13. Risk to flag: if guard runs before route_dispatcher (step 4 vs step 6), ensure prefix-match logic is identical to dispatcher — divergence means clients could hit 413 on a URL dispatcher would 404, leaking route topology. Fix: share the route-prefix matcher util between guard and dispatcher.
